name: Climb to Castillo de Castro – Sierra del Espadán
name_original: Subida al castillo de Castro — Sierra del Espadán
description:
This Route in the Sierra del Espadán is truly wonderful. Starting from the Arab town of Alfondeguilla, you have a route ahead that takes you to the Castro castle, passing through the beautiful cork oak forests of the Horcajo ravine, the arch known as L'Arquet de la Rambla and the Castro refrigerator, a well of more than 12 meters that was once used to store snow. In total, there are five refrigerators in the natural park and this is the one that is best preserved.

The itinerary is long and the last climb to the castle will test the resistance of your legs, so don't forget to bring plenty of water and a snack to recharge your batteries. Of course, when you get to the top you will see that the views are well worth the effort.

To get to Alfondeguilla, it is best to have your own vehicle, since bus connections are scarce. Remember that summer in this part of Spain can be very hot, so we recommend the rest of the seasons to face this adventure.
region_intro: Hiking around Alfondeguilla offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, a mountainous region characterized by abundant cork and pine trees. The area features gorges, natural springs like La Penyeta and El Gos, and the Belcaire river with its small pools. Trails often lead to historical sites such as Castro Castle, ancient ice houses, and old lime kilns. Best hiking trails around Alfondeguilla The most popular hiking route is Climb to Castillo de Castro – Sierra del Espadán, a 9.7 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 53 minutes to complete, leading to an Arab fortress with a commanding watchtower. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro Castle – L'Arquet of the Rambla loop from Alfondeguilla, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.5 km) path. This route combines historical exploration of Castro Castle with views of the Roman aqueduct, L'Arquet. Local hikers also love the Penya Migdia – Curved Stone loop from Alfondeguilla, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Alfondeguilla is defined by mountainous terrain within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, cork and pine forests, and historical sites like Castro Castle and L'Arquet. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to more difficult ascents. The routes in Alfondeguilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Alfondeguilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alfondeguilla?
A: Alfondeguilla offers a rich network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate walks to more challenging ascents within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alfondeguilla?
A: Hiking in Alfondeguilla immerses you in the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. Expect to traverse mountainous terrain with abundant cork and pine trees, explore gorges teeming with life, and encounter natural springs. The Belcaire river also forms small pools along some routes, adding to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate hiking options in Alfondeguilla?
A: Yes, Alfondeguilla offers a good selection of trails for different fitness levels. While there are challenging routes, you'll find 13 moderate trails and 1 easy trail. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Migdia – Curved Stone loop from Alfondeguilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 8.6 km long.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Alfondeguilla area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Alfondeguilla are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10382353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle – L'Arquet of the Rambla loop from Alfondeguilla&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21959667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Arquet of the Rambla loop from Alfondeguilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover on a hike near Alfondeguilla?
A: The trails around Alfondeguilla are rich with history. You can hike to the historic Castro Castle, an Arab fortress with a commanding watchtower, and explore the nearby Nevera de Castro, an ancient ice house. Other points of interest include old lime kilns, the cinnabar mines, and L'Arquet, a small Roman aqueduct. The San Bartolomé/Sant Vicent Cave, inhabited since the Bronze Age, is also accessible via a circular route from the village.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great panoramic views?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, consider trails leading to Penya Migdia. From its 581-meter summit, you can enjoy expansive vistas of the surrounding Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. The area around Castro Castle also offers commanding views from its elevated position.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alfondeguilla?
A: Alfondeguilla enjoys a pleasant year-round climate, making it suitable for hiking in most seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer treks. Even in winter, the climate is mild, though you should be prepared for cooler temperatures at higher elevations. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this period.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Alfondeguilla suitable for families with children?
A: While many trails involve significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for families. Look for the easier or moderate-rated routes, which tend to be shorter and less strenuous. The presence of natural springs and historical sites can also make hikes more engaging for children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Alfondeguilla?
A: The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Be mindful of other hikers and ensure your dog is well-behaved. Always carry water for your pet, particularly on longer routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trailheads in Alfondeguilla?
A: Alfondeguilla, being a rural village, typically offers parking options within or at the edge of the village, often near the starting points of popular trails. Specific parking availability can vary by trailhead, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any natural springs or water features along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its natural springs. You can discover several sources situated in gorges, such as La Penyeta, El Gos, El Flare, La Figuera, Cabanelles, and Marc. The Belcaire river also forms small, pleasant pools along some routes, offering refreshing spots.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Alfondeguilla?
A: The hiking routes in Alfondeguilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the abundance of cork and pine forests, and the historical sites like Castro Castle that are integrated into the trails.

Q: Are there any nearby natural attractions like lakes or mountain passes worth visiting?
A: Beyond the immediate hiking trails, the area around Alfondeguilla features several natural attractions. You can explore nearby lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3542279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has notable mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2477056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Nevera&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic drives or additional hiking opportunities.
tips: L'Arquet is an emblematic arch with a controversial origin (some say it is Roman and others, Arab). Through here the water was transported to the Molinet de Castro, which no longer exists, but was very close.

The Castro Castle, an Arab fortress with an impressive watchtower at a height of about 780 m, was a fundamental bastion of the Moorish resistance at the time of their expulsion. 
It was taken by the Duke of Segorbe and General Cavanilles after the Castillo de Aín had also fallen. It was also used in the Carlist Wars. 
The castle covers an area of about 2,000 m2, where two parts can be distinguished: the "Albacar", a fortified refuge for 
the inhabitants of the towns dependent on the fortress; and on the other side, the castle itself, which presided 
surrounded by a large keep that was demolished in 1938 during the Civil War. 
The views are impressively beautiful.
This refrigerator was used to store snow for the warmest months of the year.
